title: New Hybrid Workspace Guidelines & Schedule for Q3
content: We are excited to launch our updated Q3 Workspace Guidelines. Starting next month, all employees are encouraged to utilize our newly renovated office hubs in New York and Austin at least two days per week. We have added new collaboration rooms, single occupancy quiet booths, and updated our high-speed printing hubs. Please check with your team leads to align on which days your engineering or marketing teams will meet in person to optimize collaboration. Free catered lunches will continue to be provided on Tuesdays and Thursdays at both office spaces. Safe travels!
author: Sarah Jenkins (VP of HR)

title: Updated Policy: Leave Rollover Limits and Year-End Sick Leave Rules
content: Please note an important change regarding our annual time-off rollover policy. Starting this fiscal year, the maximum amount of unused Casual Leave that can be carried over into the next calendar year is capped at 5 working days. Any remaining balance beyond 5 days will lapse automatically on December 31st. Sick Leave balances will continue to reset to 10 days each year without rollover to encourage proper utilization when needed. We highly recommend reviewing your leave summary boards and planning your upcoming leaves in advance to avoid any end-of-year scheduling bottlenecks.
author: Bob Williams (HR Manager)

title: Annual Summer Tech Picnic & Hackathon Sign-up Open!
content: Get ready for our annual Summer Tech Picnic taking place on Friday, August 14th at Central Park. We will have food trucks, outdoor team games, and live music. In addition, the Hackathon committee is kickstarting signups for the 24-hour hackathon starting Thursday morning. The theme this year is "AI-driven Productivity tools for Workspaces." Teams of up to 4 can register. There are amazing prizes for 1st, 2nd, and 3rd place, including high-end mechanical keyboards, tech vouchers, and executive lunches. Click the links on our intranet calendar page to register your team before July 31st!
author: Alice Johnson (Social Committee)

title: Quarterly Town Hall & Product Roadmap Showcase
content: Join us for our Q2 Town Hall meeting on Wednesday, July 8th at 10:00 AM EST. Our executive team will share business metrics updates, user growth numbers, and our upcoming engineering objectives. We will also host a live 20-minute product showcase demonstrating the new client analytics dashboard that the team has been engineering. A calendar invite with the Zoom link has been sent to everyone. Q&A channel will open 15 minutes before the call on Slack.
author: Robert Chen (CEO)

title: New Health & Wellness Benefit Coverage Upgrades
content: We have successfully renegotiated our employee medical plans for the upcoming year, resulting in premium benefits additions without any cost increases to staff. Coverage now includes a $300 annual mental health wellness stipend, free virtual consultations for physical therapy, and a 20% discount at major fitness networks. Wellness claims can be uploaded directly through the insurance provider portal starting next Monday. Detailed benefits brochures are available for download under the resources folder.
author: Bob Williams (HR Manager)

title: Server Migration Alert: Internal Tooling Scheduled Downtime
content: Our DevOps team will be migrating several core code hosting repositories and staging servers to new cloud clusters. As a result, internal staging databases, build tools, and local credentials management pipelines will experience temporary outages on Saturday, July 11th from 01:00 AM to 06:00 AM EST. Please check in any active branches before Friday evening and contact Frank Wilson if you have any critical deployment dependencies that require custom scheduling.
author: Frank Wilson (DevOps Lead)

title: Tech Talk: Building Accessible User Interfaces with WCAG 2.2
content: Join Alice Johnson and David Miller this Thursday at 3:00 PM EST for a lunch-and-learn seminar covering the newly published WCAG 2.2 accessibility standard guidelines. They will discuss semantic HTML tree structures, keyboard focus navigation highlights, contrast optimizations in dark mode, and tools for checking screen reader tags. Food vouchers will be emailed to all remote participants who register before Tuesday afternoon.
author: David Miller (Frontend Engineer)

title: Information Security Awareness Training Due
content: Our annual Information Security compliance check is now active. All workers are required to review the updated security guidelines module and complete the quiz before June 30th. The module covers corporate phishing strategies, public Wi-Fi safety precautions, password manager hygiene, and local device encryption requirements. Failure to complete the training will result in restricted access to our repository tools.
author: Security Operations Team

title: Company-wide Hackathon Ideation Session
content: To prepare for our upcoming annual Summer Hackathon, we are hosting a collaborative brainstorming session on Slack channel #hackathon-q3. Come share raw concepts, pitches, look for teammates, and ask for technical advice. Ideas can cover AI productivity integrations, green computing initiatives, or developer experience tools. Session starts tomorrow morning at 11:00 AM EST. See you there!
author: Alice Johnson (Social Committee)

title: Celebrating Q2 Performance Milestones and Growth Updates
content: Thanks to the outstanding efforts of our engineering and customer relations teams, we have crossed our Q2 growth targets, onboarding 50+ new enterprise clients! Our system responsiveness metrics have improved by 15%, and search index query times have been halved. To celebrate this milestone, the company is hosting happy hour socials at our NY and Austin hubs this Friday evening at 5:00 PM local time. Remote workers can redeem happy hour food and beverage vouchers via Slack.
author: Robert Chen (CEO)
